title: Pit-Loss And Undercut Pressure Proxy
summary: Strategy sources emphasize the tradeoff between time lost in the pits and time gained from fresh tyres, especially undercut/overcut dynamics.
rationale: Strategy sources emphasize the tradeoff between time lost in the pits and time gained from fresh tyres, especially undercut/overcut dynamics. Current neighbor features compare tyre life/degradation, but not whether a driver has enough field gap or position pressure to justify stopping now.
implementation_hint: Within Race/LapNumber sorted by Position, derive gap proxies from LapTime differences, relative pace to cars ahead/behind, old-tyre slowdown vs recent field median, and estimated pit-loss-normalized windows by circuit/race. Add features like undercut_pressure=(pace_loss_vs_ahead + tyre_age_advantage_behind), traffic_risk in rejoin positions, and clean_air_proxy from position/lap group density.
expected_effect: Could add signal for marginal pit decisions where tyre age alone is insufficient, particularly battles in P1-P10 and high-degradation circuits.
risk: LapTime is a weak proxy for actual time gaps; position-only ordering can introduce noisy comparisons when cars are lapped or affected by safety cars/outliers.
